prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "徒手深蹲" (bodyweight squat) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the bottom of a bodyweight squat: feet shoulder-width apart and flat on the ground, knees tracking over toes, thighs roughly parallel to the floor (deep squat depth), hips pushed back, torso leaning slightly forward with neutral spine and chest up, both arms extended forward at shoulder height for balance, wearing plain black athletic shorts and a fitted gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: quadriceps (front of thighs) and gluteus maximus. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: hamstring group and erector spinae along the lower back. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "股四头肌" pointing to the quadriceps; "臀大肌" pointing to the gluteus maximus; "腘绳肌 / 竖脊肌" pointing to the hamstring and erector spinae area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
